title: Polyscias cissiflora
intro: Polyscias cissiflora, also known as the "Cissiflora" or "Polyscias," is a plant species within the Araliaceae family, native to Madagascar. The health benefits of Polyscias cissiflora include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Polyscias cissiflora is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
taxonomy: Polyscias cissiflora is classified in the kingdom Plantae. Within the kingdom, it belongs to the phylum Magnoliophyta. The phylum is further divided into the class Magnoliopsida, which is a group of flowering plants. This class is then categorized under the order Apiales, and finally, the family Araliaceae. Polyscias cissiflora is a species within the genus Polyscias, which is also part of the Araliaceae family.
